expectations.text: Uses exhaustive handling: never check, explicit return type, or Record<Status, string> mapping
expectations.evidence: Uses switch with never check in default (line 14: const _exhaustive: never = status). Explicit return type string on function.

expectations.text: No default branch that silently swallows new cases
expectations.evidence: Default branch has never check -- new union members cause compile error. Runtime fallback to gray is unreachable if types are correct.

expectations.text: Uses as const array with derived type OR explicit union type for statuses
expectations.evidence: Line 1: type PaymentStatus = 'pending' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'refunded'. Explicit union type.

expectations.text: Uses named export, not default export
expectations.evidence: Line 20-21: export { getBadgeColor }; export type { PaymentStatus }. Named exports.

assertion_critique: All 4 assertions pass for both with-skill and without-skill. These assertions are non-discriminating for this eval -- the model already knows exhaustive handling patterns.

Missing assertion: return type should be a specific union (BadgeColor) not string. With-skill returns BadgeColor, without-skill returns string. This is a meaningful quality difference the assertions do not capture.
